revisedItem.prompt: Bipolar I disorder is distinguished from Bipolar II disorder primarily by:
choices: The severity and duration of the depressive episodes
The presence of psychotic symptoms during depression
The occurrence of a full manic episode vs. only hypomanic episodes
revisedItem.rationale: The defining feature of Bipolar I is the occurrence of at least one full manic episode (lasting at least 1 week, causing marked impairment to functioning, or requiring hospitalization). If there is ever a full manic episode, the diagnosis automatically becomes Bipolar I.
